confirmed_note: Built 2026-07-19 as the post-pan segment calibration after confirming (direct pixel-intensity measurement, not eyeballing) a real ~9-13px mid-clip camera pan in data/tennis/2.mp4 between frames ~400-560 -- see PROGRESS.md camera-pan finding. Calibrated from frame 960 using wide-enough crops (BR apex confirmed at (1718,863) with a second, more-inner singles-sideline diagonal visible and correctly excluded, per cv_pipeline/scratch_output/tennis2/calib_verify_postpan/wide_BR.jpg). Re-verified across the segment at frames 560, 950, 1343 via direct pixel-intensity baseline scans (not reused from the frame-960 calibration source): BL reads (197,870)/(197,869)/(197,868) and BR reads (1718,870)/(1718,869)/(1718,868) across the 3 frames -- small real y-variance (1-2px), x constant (no horizontal pan component, independently confirmed earlier). Rendered overlay visually confirmed tight at all 4 corners on all 3 frames (calib_check_frame560/950/1343.jpg in cv_pipeline/scratch_output/tennis2/calib_verify_postpan/manifest_frames/). Held-out near-T/net-base error 4.30px/1.69px. The ~400-560 ramp frames are deliberately NOT covered by this calibration or reference_video2_calibration.py -- see PROGRESS.md for why.
